---
title: Following same preprocessing step, compare Single R and CHETAH results
date: 2024-08-15
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
execute:
  eval: false
---

# Goal of this analysis

1. Preprocess data
2. Run SingleR and predict
  a. Use $broad_w_ubc_subtypes
  b. Use $broad_cell_type
3. Run CHETAH and predict
  a. Use $broad_w_ubc_subtypes
  b. Use $broad_cell_type

A useful guide for SingleR is: https://bioconductor.org/books/release/SingleRBook/
A useful guide for CHETAH is: https://www.bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/vignettes/CHETAH/inst/doc/CHETAH_introduction.html#ref-prep

# Files and directories

## Input

CCA RL file is found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs`

Tumour dataset can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230510/mb_mnn.rds`

## Output

The output can be found at `/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240815`

# Process and Results

Lets load in the required libraries
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(Seurat)
library(qs)
library(SingleR)
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(scran)
library(CHETAH)
```

Loading in the Seurat objects.
```{r}
# Loading in Seurat objects
rl_srat <- qread("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240618/rl_cca.qs")
tumour_srat <- readRDS("/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/icheong/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230510/mb_mnn.rds")
```

Creating output directory
```{r}
set.seed(1)
out_directory <- "/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240815"
if (!dir.exists(out_directory)) {
  dir.create(out_directory, recursive = TRUE)
}
```

## Preprocessing Step

Workflow:

Subset for human cells -> For RL and tumour keep genes expressed in more than 1% of cells -> Take intersecting genes -> Normalize RNA assay -> Convert to SCE object

Subset for human cells
```{r}
rl_srat <- subset(x = rl_srat, subset = species == "human")
```

Take row sums for each gene in count matrix, keep genes expressed in 1% or more of cells
```{r}
# Get counts slot from Seurat object
counts_rl <- GetAssayData(object = rl_srat, slot = "counts")
counts_tumour <- GetAssayData(object = tumour_srat, slot = "counts")

# If a count for a gene in a cell is greater than 0, set as TRUE (= 1)
nonzero_rl <- counts_rl > 0
nonzero_tumour <- counts_tumour > 0

# If 1% or more cells are TRUE, keep the gene. Each TRUE value = 1. Taking the sum of all the cells for that gene
keep_rl_genes <- Matrix::rowSums(nonzero_rl) >= (0.01*length(Cells(rl_srat)))
keep_tumour_genes <- Matrix::rowSums(nonzero_tumour) >= (0.01*length(Cells(tumour_srat)))

# Get the genes names that we are keeping
rl_genes <- rownames(rl_srat)[keep_rl_genes]
tumour_genes <- rownames(tumour_srat)[keep_tumour_genes]
```

Take intersecting genes between the datasets
```{r}
intersecting_genes <- intersect(rl_genes, tumour_genes)  
```

```{r}
> print(length(rownames(rl_srat)))
[1] 52672

> print(length(rownames(tumour_srat)))
[1] 21624

> print(length(rl_genes))
[1] 15128

> print(length(tumour_genes))
[1] 13370

> print(length(intersecting_genes))
[1] 11077
```

Filter each dataset for intersecting genes
```{r}
rl_srat <- subset(rl_srat, features = intersecting_genes)
tumour_srat <- subset(tumour_srat, features = intersecting_genes)
```

Normalize, then converting Seurat object to SCE object
```{r}
# Normalize RNA assay before converting
rl_srat <- NormalizeData(rl_srat, assay = "RNA")
tumour_srat  <- NormalizeData(tumour_srat, assay = "RNA")

# Convert to SCE object
rl_sce <- as.SingleCellExperiment(rl_srat)
tumour_sce <- as.SingleCellExperiment(tumour_srat)
```

Saving the SCE objects
```{r}
qsave(rl_sce, file.path(out_directory, "rl_sce.qs"))
qsave(tumour_sce, file.path(out_directory, "tumour_sce.qs"))
```

## SingleR Prediction

### Using $broad_w_ubc_subtypes
```{r}
trained_model <- trainSingleR(
  rl_sce, 
  labels=rl_sce$broad_w_ubc_subtypes, 
  de.method = "wilcox",
  assay.type = "logcounts"
)

predict <- classifySingleR(
  test = tumour_sce,
  trained = trained_model,
  assay.type = "logcounts"
)
```

Now lets analyze the results.
```{r}
predict_heatmap <- plotScoreHeatmap(predict)
ggsave(file.path(out_directory, "prediction_heatmap_broad_w_ubc_subtypes.png"), plot = predict_heatmap, width = 20, height = 20, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The prediction heatmap of all the scores](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240815/prediction_heatmap_broad_w_ubc_subtypes.png)

Lets look at the raw counts for each label
```{r}
table(predict$labels)

        endothelial                  GC                 GCP           microglia 
                 21                   6                 581                 285 
oligodendrocyte/OPC                  RL               UBC_0               UBC_1 
                 42                 413                7850               12611 
              UBC_4 
               5926 
```

```{r}
predict_delta <- plotDeltaDistribution(predict, ncol = 3)
ggsave(file.path(out_directory, "prediction_delta_broad_w_ubc_subtypes.png"), plot = predict_delta, width = 20, height = 20, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The prediction delta distribution, Low deltas mean assignment is uncertain. ](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240614/prediction_delta.png)

Lets add the labels back to the tumour object
```{r}
tumour_srat@meta.data$singler_pred_label_broad_w_ubc_subtypes <- predict$labels
```

Lets create a bar plot showing the proportion of cells in each subtype
```{r}
source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")

subtype_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "singler_pred_label_broad_w_ubc_subtypes", group.by = "subtype", position = "fill") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 5), 
        legend.text = element_text(size = 5))
bar_plot_name <- "subtype_proportion_bar_plot_singleR_broad_w_ubc_subtypes.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = subtype_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 5, height = 5, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Proportion of assigned cells in each subgroup of MB. Note the lack of GCP-assignments in the SHH](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240815/subtype_proportion_bar_plot_singleR_broad_w_ubc_subtypes.png)
```{r}
table(tumour_srat$subtype, tumour_srat$singler_pred_label_broad_w_ubc_subtypes)

      endothelial   GC  GCP microglia oligodendrocyte/OPC   RL UBC_0 UBC_1
  G3           11    3    0       158                  37  153  1993  3702
  G4            9    0    0       104                   4  151  4996  5540
  SHH           1    3  581        23                   1  109   861  3369
     
      UBC_4
  G3   2588
  G4   1101
  SHH  2237
```

### Using $broad_cell_type
```{r}
trained_model <- trainSingleR(
  rl_sce, 
  labels=rl_sce$broad_cell_type, 
  de.method = "wilcox",
  assay.type = "logcounts" 
)

predict <- classifySingleR(
  test = tumour_sce,
  trained = trained_model,
  assay.type = "logcounts"
)
```

Now lets analyze the results.
```{r}
predict_heatmap <- plotScoreHeatmap(predict)
ggsave(file.path(out_directory, "prediction_heatmap_broad_cell_type.png"), plot = predict_heatmap, width = 20, height = 20, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The prediction heatmap of all the scores](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240815/prediction_heatmap_broad_cell_type.png)

Lets look at the raw counts for each label
```{r}
table(predict$labels)

        endothelial                  GC                 GCP           microglia 
                149                 250                4064                1008 
oligodendrocyte/OPC                  RL                 UBC 
                 94                9109               13061 
```

```{r}
predict_delta <- plotDeltaDistribution(predict, ncol = 3)
ggsave(file.path(out_directory, "prediction_delta_broad_cell_type.png"), plot = predict_delta, width = 20, height = 20, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![The prediction delta distribution, Low deltas mean assignment is uncertain.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240815/prediction_delta_broad_cell_type.png)

Lets add the labels back to the tumour object
```{r}
tumour_srat@meta.data$singler_pred_label_broad_cell_type <- predict$labels
```

Lets create a bar plot showing the proportion of cells in each subtype
```{r}
source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")

subtype_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "singler_pred_label_broad_cell_type", group.by = "subtype", position = "fill") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 5), 
        legend.text = element_text(size = 5))
bar_plot_name <- "subtype_proportion_bar_plot_singleR_broad_cell_type.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = subtype_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 5, height = 5, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Proportion of assigned cells in each subgroup of MB. Note there is a greater assignment of GCPs in the SHH tumour](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240815/subtype_proportion_bar_plot_singleR_broad_cell_type.png)
```{r}
table(tumour_srat$subtype, tumour_srat$singler_pred_label_broad_cell_type)

      endothelial   GC  GCP microglia oligodendrocyte/OPC   RL  UBC
  G3           46  117  119       585                  78 4304 3396
  G4           41    9   89       228                  10 3155 8373
  SHH          62  124 3856       195                   6 1650 1292
```

## CHETAH Prediction

### Using $broad_w_ubc_subtypes
```{r}
input <- CHETAHclassifier(input = tumour_sce, 
                          ref_cells = rl_sce, 
                          input_c = "logcounts",
                          ref_c = "logcounts",
                          ref_ct = "broad_w_ubc_subtypes",
                          subsample = TRUE,
                          print_steps = TRUE)
```

```{r}
png(file.path(out_directory, "chetah_correlate_reference_broad_w_ubc_subtypes.png"), width = 10, height = 10, units = "in", res=1200)
CorrelateReference(ref_cells = rl_sce, ref_c = "logcounts", ref_ct = "broad_w_ubc_subtypes")
dev.off()
```

Lets plot the classification tree
```{r}
plt <- PlotTree(input)
ggsave(filename = "chetah_classification_tree_broad_w_ubc_subtypes.png", plot = plt, path = out_directory, 
            width = 5, height = 5, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Broad_w_ubc_subtypes classification tree](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240815/chetah_classification_tree_broad_w_ubc_subtypes.png)

Lets add the labels back to the tumour object
```{r}
tumour_srat@meta.data$chetah_pred_label_broad_w_ubc_subtypes <- input$celltype_CHETAH
```

Lets look at the raw counts for each label
```{r}
table(tumour_srat$chetah_pred_label_broad_w_ubc_subtypes)

                GCP               Node6               Node7 oligodendrocyte/OPC 
                 15                 215                  11                  19 
                 RL               UBC_0               UBC_1               UBC_4 
                  1                   4               27469   
```

Lets create a bar plot showing the proportion of cells in each subtype
```{r}
source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")

subtype_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "chetah_pred_label_broad_w_ubc_subtypes", group.by = "subtype", position = "fill") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 5), 
        legend.text = element_text(size = 5))
bar_plot_name <- "subtype_proportion_bar_plot_chetah_broad_w_ubc_subtypes.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = subtype_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 5, height = 5, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Proportion of assigned cells in each subgroup of MB. Note the over prediction of UBC_1 for all MB subgroups](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240815/subtype_proportion_bar_plot_chetah_broad_w_ubc_subtypes.png)

### Using $broad_cell_type
```{r}
input <- CHETAHclassifier(input = tumour_sce, 
                          ref_cells = rl_sce, 
                          input_c = "logcounts",
                          ref_c = "logcounts",
                          ref_ct = "broad_cell_type",
                          subsample = TRUE,
                          print_steps = TRUE)
```

```{r}
png(file.path(out_directory, "chetah_correlate_reference_broad_cell_type.png"), width = 10, height = 10, units = "in", res=1200)
CorrelateReference(ref_cells = rl_sce, ref_c = "logcounts", ref_ct = "broad_cell_type")
dev.off()
```

Lets plot the classification tree
```{r}
plt <- PlotTree(input)
ggsave(filename = "chetah_classification_tree_broad_cell_type.png", plot = plt, path = out_directory, 
            width = 5, height = 5, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Broad_w_ubc_subtypes classification tree](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240815/chetah_classification_tree_broad_cell_type.png)

Lets add the labels back to the tumour object
```{r}
tumour_srat@meta.data$chetah_pred_label_broad_cell_type <- input$celltype_CHETAH
```

Lets look at the raw counts for each label
```{r}
table(tumour_srat$chetah_pred_label_broad_cell_type)

        endothelial                  GC                 GCP               Node1 
                  5                1334                4997                 165 
              Node2               Node3               Node4 oligodendrocyte/OPC 
                401                 322                5125                 280 
                 RL                 UBC          Unassigned 
                373               14729                   4
```

Lets create a bar plot showing the proportion of cells in each subtype
```{r}
source("/u/llau/software/mb_scrnaseq/MB_scRNAseq/software/utilities/cluster_barplot.R")

subtype_bar_plot <- cluster_barplot(tumour_srat, split.by = "chetah_pred_label_broad_cell_type", group.by = "subtype", position = "fill") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 5), 
        legend.text = element_text(size = 5))
bar_plot_name <- "subtype_proportion_bar_plot_chetah_broad_cell_type.png"
ggsave(filename = bar_plot_name, plot = subtype_bar_plot, path = out_directory, 
          width = 5, height = 5, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

![Proportion of assigned cells in each subgroup of MB. Note that these results are very similar to the SingleR results using the $broad_cell_type. GCPs are being predicted to be in the SHH tumour group.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/llau/results/integrated/20240815/subtype_proportion_bar_plot_chetah_broad_cell_type.png)
